title: Neural Architecture Search via Efficient Exploration
abstract: We propose a novel neural architecture search method that efficiently explores the search space using a combination of Bayesian optimization and progressive training. Our method achieves state-of-the-art results on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet while using 10x fewer GPU hours than previous methods. We demonstrate that our approach scales to large search spaces and produces architectures that transfer well across tasks.

title: On the Limitations of Large Language Models for Code Generation
abstract: We present a systematic study of the failure modes of large language models when applied to code generation tasks. Through extensive experiments on 5 benchmarks, we identify three primary categories of errors: logical reasoning failures, API misuse, and context window limitations. Our analysis reveals that scaling model size alone does not address these fundamental limitations.
